12 DEPARTMENTAL ADMINISTRATION AND SUPPORT
13 Sec. 301. The department shall notify the standard report
14 recipients when it recommends to close or consolidate any state
15 police post. The notification must include a local and state impact
16 study of the proposed post closure or consolidation.
17 Sec. 302. If the department presents a plan to the state
18 employer to privatize, the department shall submit a complete
19 project plan to the standard report recipients. The plan must
20 include the criteria under which the privatization initiative will
21 be evaluated. The evaluation must be completed and submitted to the
22 standard report recipients within 30 months after the department's
23 initial submission of the complete project plan.
24 Sec. 303. (1) When the department provides contractual
25 services to a local unit of government, the department shall be
26 reimbursed for all costs incurred in providing the services.
27 (2) The department shall define service cost models for those
28 services requiring reimbursement.
29 (3) Contractual services provided to an entity other than a
ERE H06147'26 (H-1) gbe72a

863
1 local unit of government may be provided by department personnel,
2 but only on an overtime basis outside the normal work schedule of
3 the personnel. All costs incurred in providing the services are
4 eligible for reimbursement.
5 (4) This section does not apply to services provided to state
6 agencies.
7 (5) Revenues received for contractual or reimbursed services
8 in excess of the appropriations in part 1 are appropriated and may
9 be received and expended by the department for the purposes for
10 which the funds are received.
11 (6) If additional authorization is approved in SIGMA by the
12 state budget office under this section, the department shall notify
13 the senate and house appropriations subcommittees on state police
14 and the senate and house fiscal agencies within 10 days after the
15 approval. The notification must include the amount and funding
16 source of the additional authorization, the date of its approval,
17 and the projected use of the funds to be expended.
18 Sec. 304. The department may establish and collect fees for
19 publications, videos, conferences, workshops, and related
20 materials. Fees collected under this section must be used to offset
21 expenditures for costs of the publications, videos, workshops,
22 conferences, and related materials. The department shall not
23 collect fees under this section that exceed the cost of the
24 expenditures.
25 Sec. 305. A law enforcement officer funded under part 1 shall
26 not be required to issue a predetermined or specified number of
27 citations for violations of the Michigan vehicle code, 1949 PA 300,
28 MCL 257.1 to 257.923, or of a local ordinance that substantially
29 corresponds to the provisions of the Michigan vehicle code, 1949 PA
ERE H06147'26 (H-1) gbe72a

864
1 300, MCL 257.1 to 257.923, including parking or standing
2 violations. A law enforcement officer's performance evaluation
3 system must not require a predetermined or specified number of
4 citations to be issued.
5 Sec. 306. From the funds appropriated in part 1, the director
6 shall establish and maintain local headquarters in various places,
7 and may do so by agreement, lease, or otherwise, as provided under
8 section 7 of 1935 PA 59, MCL 28.7.
9 Sec. 307. The department's investigative response team must
10 submit any compiled findings of an investigation of a critical
11 incident to the prosecutor of the county in which the critical
12 incident occurred for review. As used in this section, "critical
13 incident" means either of the following:
14 (a) Any event that results in the death or infliction of great
15 bodily harm to an employee of the department or to any other
16 individual while the individual is in the custody of a law
17 enforcement officer employed by the department or while a law
18 enforcement officer employed by the department is attempting to
19 gain control of the individual.
20 (b) Any accident involving a vehicle owned by the department
21 that was operated by an employee of the department at the time of
22 the accident that results in the death or infliction of great
23 bodily harm to the employee or any other individual involved in the
24 accident.

1 Sec. 403. (1) The department shall provide forensic testing

12 turnaround time across all forensic science disciplines.

1 $400,000.00 to create and administer a competitive grant program to

6 subsection on a schedule as determined by the department.
7 (11) A county that is awarded a grant under subsection (10)
8 shall use the funds only to implement critical incident mapping
9 that satisfies all of the following:
10 (a) Is compatible with platforms and applications used by
11 local, state, and federal public safety officials.
12 (b) Does not require the purchase of additional software for
13 use.
14 (c) Is provided in a printable format.
15 (d) Is verified for accuracy through a walk-through of a
16 building and grounds.
17 (e) Is oriented true north.
18 (f) Includes accurate floor plan information overlaid on
19 current aerial imagery of a building.
20 (g) Includes site-specific labeling that matches the structure
21 of the building, including room labels, hallway names, external
22 door or stairwell numbers, locations of hazards, key utility
23 locations, key boxes, automated external defibrillators, and trauma
24 kits.
25 (h) Includes site-specific labeling that matches the building
26 grounds, including parking areas, athletic fields, surrounding
27 roads, and neighboring properties.
28 (i) Includes a gridded overlay with x/y coordinates.
29 (j) Includes information that best assists first responders in

15 report any information that could compromise the security of any
16 critical infrastructure.
17 (17) Revenue collected by the department under this section
18 for the emergency management and homeland security training center
19 that is unexpended and unencumbered at the end of the fiscal year
20 must not lapse to the general fund, but must be carried forward
21 into the subsequent fiscal year.
22 (18) As used in this section:
23 (a) "Critical infrastructure" means a facility designated as
24 critical infrastructure by this state, or that is part of a
25 critical infrastructure sector identified by the federal
26 Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency.
27 (b) "Drone" means an unmanned aircraft flown by a remote pilot
28 via a ground control system, or autonomously through use of an
29 onboard computer, communication links, and any additional equipment
ERE H06147'26 (H-1) gbe72a

884
1 that is necessary for the unmanned aircraft to operate safely.
2 (c) "Drone-as-first-responder program" means a system that can
3 remotely deploy 1 or more drones from strategic locations to the
4 site of a public safety incident for the purpose of providing real-
5 time information to law enforcement officers.
6 (d) "Drone operations management system" means a centralized
7 platform that is used by a drone operator to manage drone missions,
8 operations, assets, and data.
9 (e) "Drone readiness program" means a program designed to
10 prepare local units of government for the safe, compliant, and
11 efficient operation of drones.
12 (f) "Key facility" means that term as defined in section 552c
13 of the Michigan penal code, 1931 PA 328, MCL 750.552c.
14 (g) "Local unit of government" means a county, city, township,
15 or village.
16 (h) "Unmanned traffic management system" means a centralized
17 platform that is used to authorize, coordinate, and monitor low-
18 altitude drone operations conducted by multiple drone operators for
19 the purposes of ensuring airspace safety, deconfliction, and
20 regulatory compliance.
21 Sec. 605. The department shall provide for the planning,
22 administration, and implementation of highway traffic safety
23 programs to save lives and reduce injuries on roads in this state,
24 in partnership with other public and private organizations.
25 Sec. 606. (1) Funds appropriated in part 1 for the secondary
26 road patrol program must be used to provide grants to sheriffs
27 under the secondary road patrol program described under section 76
28 of 1846 RS 14, MCL 51.76.
29 (2) The sheriffs' duties under the secondary road patrol
ERE H06147'26 (H-1) gbe72a

885
1 program, as outlined in section 76(2) of 1846 RS 14, MCL 51.76, are
2 to do all of the following:
3 (a) Patrol and monitor traffic violations.
4 (b) Enforce the criminal laws of this state, violations of
5 which are observed by or brought to the attention of the sheriff's
6 department while patrolling and monitoring secondary roads.
7 (c) Investigate accidents involving motor vehicles.
8 (d) Provide emergency assistance to persons on or near a
9 highway or road the sheriff is patrolling and monitoring.
10 Sec. 607. (1) The department shall serve as an active liaison
11 between the department of technology, management, and budget and
12 state, local, regional, and federal public safety agencies on
13 matters pertaining to the Michigan public safety communications
14 system and shall report user issues to the department of
15 technology, management, and budget.
16 (2) Not later than January 15, the department, in cooperation
17 with the department of technology, management, and budget, shall
18 submit a report to the standard report recipients on the status of
19 the Michigan public safety communications system that includes, but
20 is not limited to, all of the following information:
21 (a) Planned or anticipated upgrades or maintenance to the
22 Michigan public safety communications system.
23 (b) Upgrades or maintenance required to be in compliance with
24 applicable federal or state law, including the date by which the
25 upgrades or maintenance is expected to be completed or must be
26 completed under applicable federal or state law.
27 (c) The estimated cost of the upgrades or maintenance projects
28 included in the report under this subsection.
29
ERE H06147'26 (H-1) gbe72a

886
1 ONE-TIME APPROPRIATIONS
2 Sec. 701. (1) From the funds appropriated in part 1 for cold
3 case investigations, the department shall create and administer a
4 competitive grant program that provides grants not to exceed
5 $200,000.00 to Michigan universities that operate a cold case
6 program. As used in this section, "cold case program" means an
7 academic program administered by a university that provides, but is
8 not limited to, workforce development training related to criminal
9 investigation tactics, forensic science and law, and review of cold
10 homicide and missing persons cases.
11 (2) The department shall not approve a grant application under
12 this section if a university does not satisfy either of the
13 following:
14 (a) The university has operated a cold case program with the
15 department for over a year as of October 1, 2024.
16 (b) The university will begin a cold case program with the
17 department not later than October 1, 2027.
18 (3) Funds disbursed under this section must be used only for
19 programmatic and operational expenses of the university's cold case
20 program.
21 Sec. 702. (1) From the funds appropriated in part 1 for law
22 enforcement training for communication with limited English
23 speaking communities and those deaf and hard of hearing, the
24 department grants and community services division shall create and
25 administer a block grant program to support training provided by an
26 entity holding an oral transliteration certificate. The oral
27 transliteration certificate must be held by an officer, board
28 member, or principal of the entity and must have been held for not
29 less than 1 year. Any training course provided for under this
ERE H06147'26 (H-1) gbe72a

887
1 section must be certified by the International Accreditors for
2 Continuing Education and Training. The certification required under
3 this section may be obtained and maintained through a developer who
4 has current status with the International Accreditors for
5 Continuing Education and Training. The purpose of the grant is to
6 train officers to better assist in their communication with members
7 of the public who experience a language barrier or may be hard of
8 hearing or deaf.
9 (2) The unexpended funds appropriated in part 1 for law
10 enforcement training for communication with limited English
11 speaking communities and those deaf and hard of hearing are
12 designated as a work project appropriation, and any unencumbered or
13 unallotted funds must not lapse at the end of the fiscal year and
14 must be available for expenditures for projects under this section
15 until the projects have been completed. The following is in
16 compliance with section 451a(1) of the management and budget act,
17 1984 PA 431, MCL 18.145a:
18 (a) The purpose of the project is the training of law
19 enforcement officers.
20 (b) The project will be accomplished by utilizing contracts
21 with service providers.
22 (c) The estimated cost of this project is $500,000.00.
23 (d) The tentative completion date for the work project is
24 September 30, 2029.
25 (3) The department may use the funds appropriated in part 1
26 for law enforcement training for communicating with limited English
27 speaking communities and those deaf and hard of hearing for costs
28 associated with the administration and oversight of the block grant
29 program.

13 Sec. 204. If the state administrative board, acting under
14 section 3 of 1921 PA 2, MCL 17.3, transfers funds from an amount
15 appropriated under part 1, the legislature may, by a concurrent
16 resolution adopted by a majority of the members elected to and
17 serving in each house, intertransfer funds within part 1 for the
18 particular department, board, commission, officer, or institution.
19 Sec. 205. (1) The department shall use the internet to fulfill
20 the reporting requirements of this part and shall make each report
21 readily accessible to the public and conspicuously post each
22 required report in a single archivable location on the department's
23 website not later than the due date required for each report.
24 (2) In addition to placing all reports required in the current
25 fiscal year on the department's website, the department shall
26 maintain on its website all reports placed on the website from
27 previous fiscal years posted by fiscal year in the same single
28 archivable location.
29 (3) The department shall transmit all required reports for the
ERE H06147'26 (H-1) gbe72a

902
1 current fiscal year to the standard report recipients and any other
2 required recipients by email. The email shall include a copy of the
3 report and a link to access the report online.
4 Sec. 206. The department shall receive and retain copies of
5 all reports funded from appropriations in part 1. The department
6 shall follow federal and state law and guidelines for short-term
7 and long-term retention of records. The department shall
8 electronically retain copies of reports unless otherwise required
9 by federal and state guidelines.
10 Sec. 207. (1) The department shall cooperate with the
11 department of technology, management, and budget to maintain a
12 searchable website accessible by the public at no cost that
13 includes, but is not limited to, all of the following for the
14 department:
15 (a) Fiscal year-to-date expenditures by category.
16 (b) Fiscal year-to-date expenditures by appropriation unit.
17 (c) Fiscal year-to-date payments to a selected vendor,
18 including the vendor name, payment date, payment amount, and
19 payment description.
20 (2) The department shall cooperate with the department of
21 technology, management, and budget to update the searchable website
22 on a quarterly basis.
23 Sec. 208. Not later than December 15, the state budget office
24 shall prepare and submit a report that provides estimates of the
25 total general fund/general purpose appropriation lapses at the
26 close of the previous fiscal year. The report must summarize the
27 projected year-end general fund/general purpose appropriation
28 lapses by major departmental program or program areas.
29 Sec. 209. Not later than 14 days after the release of the
ERE H06147'26 (H-1) gbe72a

903
1 executive budget recommendation, the department shall cooperate
2 with the state budget office to provide an annual report on

6 Sec. 210. Not later than November 15, the department shall
7 report on private and other third-party funds received by the
8 department in the previous fiscal year. The report must include the
9 amount of funding received, the specific source of funding
10 received, the purpose for which funding was expended, and the
11 amount of any remaining funds.
12 Sec. 211. Consistent with section 217 of the management and
13 budget act, 1984 PA 431, MCL 18.1217, the department shall prepare
14 a report on out-of-state travel expenses by not later than January
15 1. The report must list all travel outside this state by classified
16 and unclassified employees in the previous fiscal year that was
17 funded in whole or in part with funds appropriated in the
18 department's budget. The report must include all of the following
19 information:
20 (a) The dates of each travel occurrence.
21 (b) The total transportation and related costs of each travel
22 occurrence and the proportions funded with state general
23 fund/general purpose revenues, state restricted revenues, federal
24 revenues, local revenues, and private revenues, including specific
25 sources of state restricted, federal, local, and private revenues.
26 (c) The destination of each travel occurrence.
27 (d) The specific purpose and description of each travel
28 occurrence, including the specific name of the event or meeting.
29 Sec. 212. On a quarterly basis, the department shall report on
ERE H06147'26 (H-1) gbe72a

904
1 all of the following:
2 (a) The number of full-time equated positions in pay status by
3 civil service classification.
4 (b) A comparison by line item of the number of full-time
5 equated positions authorized from funds appropriated in part 1 to
6 the actual number of full-time equated positions employed by the
7 department at the end of the reporting period.

10 (i) Full-time salary.

18 (ix) Job share.

21 classifications.
22 Sec. 213. Not later than April 1, the department shall report
23 on each specific policy change made to implement a public act
24 affecting the department that took effect during the previous
25 calendar year. The report must include reference to the public act

20 department shall keep that bid documentation confidential and shall
21 not disclose that bid documentation other than to a department
22 representative without the contractor's written consent. The
23 department may disclose the bid documentation if necessary to
24 address or defend a claim by a contractor.

14 PA 51, MCL 247.660b. Proceeds received by this state from the sale

18 deposits not returned to a lessee of state-owned intercity bus

20 the intercity bus equipment and facility fund for the repair of

22 department from lease payments for state-owned intercity bus
23 equipment, and facility maintenance charges under terms of leases
24 of state-owned intercity facilities, must be credited to the
25 intercity bus equipment and facility fund for the purchase and
26 repair of intercity bus equipment or for the maintenance and

29 in the intercity bus equipment and facility fund remain in the fund

1 and are carried forward into the succeeding fiscal year.
2 Sec. 702. Money that is received by this state as repayment

4 result of the sale of property or equipment used or projected to be
5 used for rail or water freight projects must be deposited in the

7 preservation act of 1976, 1976 PA 295, MCL 474.67. At the close of

10 fiscal year.

25 pursuant to section 8 of the state transportation preservation act
26 of 1976, 1976 PA 295, MCL 474.58, the department shall immediately
27 notify the senate and house appropriations subcommittees on
28 transportation and the state budget office that the railroad
29 company has filed with the appropriate governmental agencies for

1 abandonment of a line.
2 Sec. 706. The Detroit/Wayne County Port Authority shall issue
3 a complete operations assessment and a financial disclosure
4 statement. The operations assessment shall include operational
5 goals for the next 5 years and recommendations to improve land
6 acquisition and development efficiency. The report shall be
7 completed and submitted to the house of representatives and senate
8 appropriations subcommittees on transportation, the state budget
9 director, and the house and senate fiscal agencies by June 30 of
10 each fiscal year for the prior fiscal year.
11 Sec. 707. (1) Before March 1, 2027, the department shall
12 provide to the legislature, the state budget office, and the senate
13 and house fiscal agencies its rail strategic plan. The strategic
14 plan must include, but is not limited to, a rolling 5-year rail
15 plan and a summary of the department's obligations for programs
16 funded under the appropriation in part 1 for rail operations and
17 infrastructure.
18 (2) The rolling 5-year rail plan must include, but is not
19 limited to, all of the following:
20 (a) A listing by county of all rail infrastructure projects on
21 rail lines within this state utilizing state funds, and the
22 estimated cost of each project.
23 (b) The actual or projected state expenditures for operation
24 of passenger rail service.
25 (c) The actual or projected state expenditures for maintenance
26 of passenger service rail lines.
27 (3) The period of the rolling 5-year rail plan must include
28 the fiscal year ending September 30, 2027 and the immediately
29 following 4 fiscal years.

1 (4) The summary of the department's obligations for programs
2 funded under the appropriation in part 1 for rail operations and
3 infrastructure must include a breakdown of the appropriation by
4 program, year-to-year obligations under each program itemized by
5 project, and an estimate of future obligations under each program
6 itemized by project for the remainder of the fiscal year.
7 (5) From the funds appropriated in part 1 for rail operation
8 and infrastructure, not less than $20,000,000.00 must be allocated
9 for the support of rail-related economic development projects and
10 rail freight system preservation projects.
11 Sec. 719. The department must allow access to department-owned
12 park and ride facilities by public transit agency-owned vehicles
13 for the purposes of passenger boarding and disembarking from public
14 transportation vehicles.
15 Sec. 721. The part 1 appropriation for maritime and port
16 facility program must be used for grants as provided under section
17 7 of the maritime and port facility assistance grant program act,
18 2022 PA 159, MCL 120.157. Priority must be given to projects
19 meeting the allowable uses of section 5(1)(c), (d), and (e) of the
20 maritime and port facility assistance grant program act, 2022 PA
21 159, MCL 120.155.
22 Sec. 722. In addition to the appropriations in part 1, for the
23 fiscal year ending September 30, 2027, there is appropriated the
24 following:
25 (a) From the comprehensive transportation fund created in
26 section 10b of 1951 PA 51, MCL 247.660b, $7,735,200.00 for credit

28 the state of Michigan, 1945 PA 327, MCL 259.1 to 259.208.

1 section 10b of 1951 PA 51, MCL 247.660b, $6,585,000.00 for credit

4 Sec. 724. The part 1 appropriation for air service program –
5 general aviation airports must be used for capital improvement
6 grants and air service improvements at general aviation airports in
7 this state.
8 Sec. 735. For the fiscal year ending September 30, 2027, the

10 1951 PA 51, MCL 247.660e, is $0.00.
11
12 AERONAUTICS FUND
13 Sec. 801. Except as otherwise provided in section 903 of this
14 part for capital outlay, at the close of the fiscal year, any
15 unobligated and unexpended balance in the state aeronautics fund
16 created in the aeronautics code of the state of Michigan, 1945 PA
17 327, MCL 259.1 to 259.208, lapses to the state aeronautics fund and
18 may be appropriated by the legislature in the immediately
19 succeeding fiscal year.
20 Sec. 802. From the funds appropriated in part 1 for aviation
21 services, the department shall retain and maintain air service
22 between Marquette and Lansing with priority service as follows:
23 (a) Emergencies.
24 (b) Routine travel for elected state officials.
25 (c) Other uses.
26
27 CAPITAL OUTLAY
28 Sec. 901. (1) From federal-state-local project appropriations
29 contained in part 1 for the purpose of assisting political entities
ERE H06147'26 (H-1) gbe72a

927
1 and subdivisions of this state in the construction and improvement
2 of publicly used airports and landing fields within this state, the
3 state transportation department may permit the award of contracts
4 on behalf of units of local government for the authorized locations
5 not to exceed the indicated amounts, of which the state allocated
6 portion must not exceed the amount appropriated in part 1.
7 (2) Political entities and subdivisions shall provide not less
8 than 5% of the cost of any project under this section, unless a
9 total nonfederal share less than 10% is otherwise specified in
10 federal law. State money must not be allocated until local money is
11 allocated. State money for any 1 project must not exceed 1/3 of the
12 total appropriation in part 1 from state funds for airport
13 improvement programs.
14 (3) The Michigan aeronautics commission may take those steps
15 necessary to match federal money available for airport construction
16 and improvement within this state and to meet the matching
17 requirements of the federal government. Whether acting alone or
18 jointly with another political subdivision or public agency or with
19 this state, a political subdivision or public agency of this state
20 shall not submit to any agency of the federal government a project
21 application for airport planning or development unless it is
22 authorized in this part and part 1 and the project application is
23 approved by the governing body of each political subdivision or
24 public agency making the application and by the Michigan
25 aeronautics commission.
26 Sec. 903. The appropriations in part 1 for capital outlay are
27 carried forward at the end of the fiscal year consistent with the
28 provisions of section 248 of the management and budget act, 1984 PA
29 431, MCL 18.1248.

1
2 ONE-TIME APPROPRIATIONS
3 Sec. 1001. The part 1 appropriation for rail freight economic
4 development must be used for grants in support of rail freight and
5 rail freight economic development programs in this state.
6 Sec. 1002. The part 1 appropriation for grants to commercial
7 service airports must be used for grants for capital improvements
8 at commercial service airports in this state with fewer than
9 5,000,000 enplanements each year.
